harnessEngineering.source: OpenAI Harness Engineering (2025) — applied to this project
harnessEngineering.corePhilosophy: Humans steer. Agents execute. The engineer's job is to design environments, specify intent, and build feedback loops — not write code directly.
agentFirstPrinciples: AGENTS.md (and CLAUDE.md) is the TABLE OF CONTENTS, not the encyclopedia. Keep it under 100 lines. It points to deeper docs in docs/. Never put all context in one file.
Repository is the SYSTEM OF RECORD. All architectural decisions, design choices, patterns, and conventions must live as versioned markdown in docs/. If it is not in the repo, the agent cannot see it.
Progressive disclosure: agents start with CLAUDE.md as a map, follow links to relevant docs/, and are never overwhelmed up front.
Enforce invariants mechanically. Custom linters with remediation instructions in error messages. CI jobs validate the knowledge base is up to date. Structural tests catch layer violations.

Layered domain architecture: within each backend domain, dependencies flow forward only — Types → Config → Repo → Service → Runtime → API. Cross-cutting concerns (auth, telemetry, feature flags) enter through explicit Providers. Enforced by linters.
Agent legibility over human style. The codebase is optimised so an agent can reason about the full business domain from the repo itself. Prefer composable, stable, well-documented dependencies.
Golden principles encoded in docs/design-docs/core-beliefs.md. A recurring background task (ralph) scans for deviations and opens targeted fix-up pull requests.

Execution plans are first-class artifacts. Active plans in docs/exec-plans/active/, completed in docs/exec-plans/completed/, known debt in docs/exec-plans/tech-debt-tracker.md. All versioned in the repo.
Quality grading in docs/QUALITY_SCORE.md — grades each product domain and architectural layer, tracks gaps over time.
Ephemeral observability per git worktree. Each ralph iteration boots an isolated app instance with its own logs and metrics. Phoenix traces are worktree-scoped.
Technical debt as garbage collection. Pay it down continuously with automated small PRs, not in painful bursts.
Parse and validate data at every boundary. Never probe data YOLO-style. Always validate inputs with Pydantic (backend) or Zod (frontend).
Boring technology wins. Well-known, composable, API-stable libraries are easier for agents to reason about. Avoid opaque upstream dependencies where a focused reimplementation would be more legible.
Throughput changes the merge philosophy. Minimal blocking merge gates. Short-lived PRs. Corrections are cheap; waiting is expensive.

repositoryStructure.CLAUDE.md: Table of contents only (~100 lines). Points to docs/. Contains the 10 most critical invariants. Updated by ralph when patterns change.
repositoryStructure.ARCHITECTURE.md: Top-level domain map. Package layering diagram. Updated when new domains are added.
repositoryStructure.docs/design-docs/: index.md + core-beliefs.md + per-feature design decisions with verification status.
repositoryStructure.docs/exec-plans/active/: Current execution plans with progress logs checked in.
repositoryStructure.docs/product-specs/: index.md + per-feature specs referenced by stories.
repositoryStructure.docs/references/: LLM-friendly plain-text reference docs for key dependencies: uv-llms.txt, lancedb-llms.txt, langgraph-llms.txt, kuzu-llms.txt, fsrs-llms.txt.
repositoryStructure.docs/generated/: Auto-generated artifacts: db-schema.md (regenerated on migration), api-schema.md.
repositoryStructure.docs/QUALITY_SCORE.md: Quality grade per domain. Updated by ralph after each phase.
repositoryStructure.docs/RELIABILITY.md: SLOs and reliability requirements per critical path.
repositoryStructure.docs/SECURITY.md: Security boundaries and data handling rules.
repositoryStructure.docs/FRONTEND.md: Frontend conventions, component patterns, state management rules.

ciPrinciples: Every commit must pass: uv run pytest (backend), npx tsc --noEmit + npm run build (frontend), ruff check + ruff format --check (backend linting), custom architectural layer linter.
CI validates the docs/ knowledge base: checks that ARCHITECTURE.md cross-links are live, QUALITY_SCORE.md is not stale, exec-plans reference real story IDs.
Linter error messages include remediation instructions so ralph can self-fix without human intervention.
No human PR review required by default. Agent-to-agent review loop. Humans review only when judgment is required.
Flaky tests are addressed with a follow-up PR, not by blocking the current one indefinitely.

techStack.projectLayout: Monorepo root: /backend (Python FastAPI), /frontend (React+Vite+TypeScript), /evals (RAGAS evaluation scripts), /docs (system of record per Harness Engineering principles)

backend.dependencies: uv for ALL package management. Never use pip, Poetry, or pipenv. Commands: uv init, uv add <pkg>, uv sync, uv run <cmd>, uv lock. Lockfile: uv.lock committed to repo. pyproject.toml uses [project] table (PEP 621). Dev dependencies in [dependency-groups] dev section.
backend.workflow: LangGraph — content-type routing, ingestion pipeline, RAG pipeline. Replace any LangChain chains with LangGraph nodes.
backend.documentParsing: PyMuPDF (fitz) for PDF/DOCX/XLSX/PPTX; unstructured.io for complex mixed layouts; chardet for encoding detection
backend.chunking: LangGraph node: RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter with per-type sizes — papers: 300 tokens 15% overlap, books: 600 tokens 20% overlap, conversations: 450 tokens 20% overlap, notes/diary: 300 tokens 25% overlap, code: function/class boundaries via tree-sitter
backend.embeddings: BAAI/bge-m3 via sentence-transformers with ONNX Runtime acceleration (optimum[onnxruntime]). 1024-dim vectors. Cache model in DATA_DIR/models/
backend.vectorDB: LanceDB — embedded, Apache Arrow backend, .lance files in DATA_DIR/vectors/
backend.keywordIndex: SQLite FTS5 virtual table on chunks.text — provides BM25 keyword retrieval
backend.graphDB: Kuzu — embedded analytical graph DB (like DuckDB but for graphs). Apache 2.0. Cypher query language. DATA_DIR/graph.kuzu
backend.hybridRetrieval: Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) combining vector (LanceDB cosine) + keyword (SQLite FTS5 BM25) + graph (Kuzu entity traversal). k=60 in RRF formula.
backend.ner: GLiNER (urchade/gliner_multi_pii-v1 or gliner-multi-v2.1) — zero-shot NER with custom entity types: PERSON, ORG, PLACE, CONCEPT, EVENT, TECHNOLOGY, DATE
backend.llmUnified: LiteLLM as unified LLM API layer. All LLM calls go through LiteLLM — never call provider SDKs directly. Supports: Ollama (local), OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini.
backend.localLLM: Ollama with llama.cpp backend. Default models: mistral:7b (summarization, Q&A), phi3:mini (lightweight classification). Auto-pull if missing.
backend.spacedRepetition: FSRS algorithm (fsrs Python package). Replaces SM-2 entirely.
backend.tracing: Arize Phoenix (arize-phoenix) running as a local server on port 6006. Instrument all LangGraph nodes and LLM calls with OpenTelemetry via phoenix.otel.
backend.evaluation: Langfuse (self-hosted via Docker Compose) + RAGAS for automated RAG metrics. Langfuse stores golden datasets per content type. RAGAS runs evals on every golden dataset.
backend.metadataDB: SQLite 3 via SQLAlchemy (async). Single file: DATA_DIR/luminary.db

frontend.ui: shadcn/ui components + Tailwind CSS. Run `npx shadcn-ui@latest init` during scaffold.
frontend.graphViz: Sigma.js v3 + Graphology v0.25. WebGL-accelerated. Handles 10K+ nodes at 60fps.
frontend.animation: Framer Motion — flashcard flips, tab transitions, progress animations
frontend.state: Zustand for global state (current document, LLM mode, study session)
frontend.dataFetching: TanStack Query (React Query) v5 — caching, background refetch, streaming
frontend.charts: Recharts for progress/retention curves and monitoring metrics

techStack.desktop: Tauri v1.6 (Apache 2.0). Added in Phase 5 after core web app is stable. /desktop directory.
techStack.cloudScalingPath: LanceDB -> Qdrant, SQLite -> PostgreSQL, Ollama -> vLLM, single instance -> Docker Compose. Keep service boundaries clean for this migration.

tabs.label: Learning
tabs.description: Document reader + inline notes linked to passages. Text-selection toolbar for explain/simplify/add-note. Glossary drawer. Content library accessible from this tab.

tabs.label: Chat
tabs.description: Conversational Q&A. Scope selector: current document or all content. Streaming answers with citation chips and confidence badges. Conversation history sidebar.

tabs.label: Viz
tabs.description: Interactive knowledge graph via Sigma.js. Filter by entity type and document source. Node click shows detail popover. Cross-document graph toggle.

tabs.label: Study
tabs.description: FSRS flashcard review. Learning modes: Flashcard, Teach-back, Socratic, Synthesis. Progress dashboard: retention curves, mastery heatmap, study streak, weak areas, misconception tracker.

tabs.label: Monitoring
tabs.description: RAG metrics (HR@5, MRR, Faithfulness, Context Precision). Phoenix traces for recent queries. Langfuse eval run results per golden dataset. Model usage breakdown (local vs cloud, latency). Ingestion queue status.

stories.title: Backend monorepo scaffold — uv, Python 3.13, FastAPI app skeleton
stories.description: Create the backend half of the monorepo. At repo root: create .gitignore covering .venv, node_modules, .env, __pycache__, *.pyc, .lance, *.db, DATA_DIR. Create README.md with setup in 3 commands (cd backend && uv sync, cd frontend && npm install, make dev). Create /evals directory with evals/README.md placeholder. BACKEND (/backend): Run `uv init` to initialise the project. Edit pyproject.toml — set [project] name='luminary-backend', version='0.1.0', requires-python='>=3.13'. Run `uv add fastapi 'uvicorn[standard]' pydantic-settings`. Run `uv add --dev pytest pytest-asyncio httpx ruff`. Add [tool.ruff] to pyproject.toml: line-length=100, target-version='py313', select=['E','F','I','UP']. Create app/__init__.py (empty). Create app/main.py with a minimal FastAPI app: app = FastAPI(title='Luminary') and a GET /health endpoint returning {"status": "ok", "version": "1.0.0"}. Create app/config.py with a Settings class (pydantic-settings BaseSettings): DATA_DIR (default '~/.luminary'), OLLAMA_URL (default 'http://localhost:11434'), LOG_LEVEL (default 'INFO'), LITELLM_DEFAULT_MODEL (default 'ollama/mistral'), PHOENIX_ENABLED (bool, default True), OPENAI_API_KEY (str, default ''), ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (str, default ''), GOOGLE_API_KEY (str, default ''), LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY (str, default ''), LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY (str, default ''). Create tests/__init__.py (empty). Create a Makefile at repo root with targets: dev (runs both servers), ci (placeholder for now: echo 'CI not yet configured').

stories.title: Frontend scaffold — Vite+React+TS, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, 5 tab stubs
stories.description: Create the frontend half of the monorepo. FRONTEND (/frontend): Run `npm create vite@latest frontend -- --template react-ts` from the repo root. cd frontend. Install Tailwind CSS v3: `npm install -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer` and `npx tailwindcss init -p`. Configure tailwind.config.js content to include './src/**/*.{ts,tsx}'. Add Tailwind directives to src/index.css. Initialise shadcn/ui: `npx shadcn-ui@latest init` — choose New York style, zinc base color, enable CSS variables. Install runtime deps: `npm install react-router-dom@6 @tanstack/react-query@5 zustand framer-motion`. Create src/pages/Learning.tsx, Chat.tsx, Viz.tsx, Study.tsx, Monitoring.tsx — each exports a React component that renders a centered heading with the tab name (placeholder only). Create src/store.ts with a Zustand store: useAppStore with fields: activeDocumentId (string | null), llmMode ('local' | 'cloud' | 'unavailable'), and setActiveDocument(id: string | null) action. Update src/App.tsx: wrap the app in QueryClientProvider and BrowserRouter. Render a persistent left sidebar (64px wide, full height, shadcn bg-sidebar) with 5 icon buttons routing to /, /chat, /viz, /study, /monitoring. Use React Router Routes to render the correct page component. Ensure TypeScript strict mode in tsconfig.json (strict: true).

stories.title: Document parsing — PDF, DOCX, TXT, Markdown with section detection
stories.description: Implement document format parsing in app/services/parser.py. Add: `uv add pymupdf python-docx markdown-it-py chardet`. Define dataclasses in app/types.py: Section(heading: str, level: int, text: str, page_start: int, page_end: int) and ParsedDocument(title: str, format: str, pages: int, word_count: int, sections: list[Section], raw_text: str). Create DocumentParser class with method parse(file_path: Path, format: str) -> ParsedDocument. PDF (fitz): open with fitz.open(), extract text page by page, detect headings by font size > body_avg * 1.2 heuristic. DOCX (python-docx): iterate paragraphs, detect Heading 1/2/3 by paragraph.style.name. TXT: chardet for encoding detection, split on double-newline for sections. MD (markdown-it-py): parse headings (#, ##, ###) for section boundaries. Store original file to DATA_DIR/raw/{document_id}.{ext} before parsing. Create POST /documents/parse endpoint (multipart file upload, returns ParsedDocument as JSON). Create tests/test_parser.py with fixture files (minimal 2-page PDF using reportlab, small DOCX, a TXT, a MD file) and tests for each format. Add `uv add --dev reportlab` for test fixture generation.

stories.title: LangGraph ingestion workflow — parse, classify, chunk nodes + POST /ingest
stories.description: Build the core LangGraph ingestion workflow in app/workflows/ingestion.py. Add: `uv add langgraph langchain-core langchain-text-splitters`. Define IngestionState as TypedDict: document_id (str), file_path (str), format (str), parsed_document (dict | None), content_type (str | None), chunks (list | None), status (str), error (str | None). Build a StateGraph with these nodes: (1) parse_node: calls DocumentParser.parse(), stores result in state. (2) classify_node: heuristics only — conversation if regex finds 'Speaker:' or timestamp patterns, paper if 'abstract' or 'methodology' heading present, book if chapter headings and word_count > 40000, code if .py/.js/.ts/.go extension, else notes. Default 'notes' when ambiguous. No LiteLLM call here — LiteLLM classification enhancement comes in a later story. (3) chunk_node: RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter per content_type — paper: chunk_size=300 overlap=45, book: chunk_size=600 overlap=120, conversation: chunk_size=450 overlap=90, notes/code: chunk_size=300 overlap=75. Stores chunks in SQLite chunks table with a new AsyncSession. (4) embed_node: stub — log 'embedding pending (S05)', update document stage to 'embedding'. (5) keyword_index_node: stub — log 'keyword index pending (S06)', update stage to 'indexing'. (6) entity_extract_node: stub — log 'entity extraction pending (S15b)', update stage to 'complete'. Graph edges: START -> parse -> classify -> chunk -> embed -> keyword_index -> entity_extract -> END. Embed and keyword_index can stay sequential for now (parallel wiring in a later story). Create app/routers/documents.py: POST /ingest (multipart form: file), save file to DATA_DIR/raw/, create DocumentModel in SQLite (stage='parsing'), launch workflow as asyncio background task via asyncio.create_task(), return {document_id, status: 'processing'}. GET /documents/{id}/status returns {stage, progress_pct, done}. progress_pct: parsing=10, classifying=25, chunking=40, embedding=70, indexing=90, complete=100. Include this router in main.py.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /ingest returns document_id and status='processing' immediately

stories.title: SQLite FTS5 keyword index and hybrid retrieval with RRF
stories.description: Implement keyword indexing and hybrid retrieval. Update ingestion workflow keyword_index_node (replacing stub in S04): after chunks are in SQLite, run INSERT INTO chunks_fts(rowid, text, chunk_id, document_id) SELECT rowid, text, id, document_id FROM chunks WHERE document_id = ?. Update document stage to 'indexing'. Define ScoredChunk dataclass in app/types.py: chunk_id, document_id, text, section_heading, page, score (float), source (Literal['vector','keyword','both']). Create app/services/retriever.py with HybridRetriever class: vector_search(query: str, document_ids: list[str] | None, k: int) -> list[ScoredChunk]: embed query with EmbeddingService, call LanceDB table.search().metric('cosine').limit(k), filter by document_ids if provided. keyword_search(query: str, document_ids: list[str] | None, k: int) -> list[ScoredChunk]: run SELECT chunk_id, document_id, text, bm25(chunks_fts) AS score FROM chunks_fts WHERE chunks_fts MATCH ? ORDER BY score LIMIT ?. rrf_merge(vector_results, keyword_results, k: int = 10) -> list[ScoredChunk]: for each result list assign ranks, compute rrf_score = sum(1/(60+rank)), merge dicts keyed by chunk_id, sort descending, return top k. retrieve(query, document_ids, k) -> list[ScoredChunk]: vector_search(k=20) + keyword_search(k=20) -> rrf_merge(k=k). Write tests/test_retriever.py: test each method with mocked LanceDB and SQLite returns, test rrf_merge merges and ranks correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: keyword_index_node replaces stub — FTS5 populated after ingestion

stories.title: LiteLLM integration — Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini with streaming
stories.description: Set up LiteLLM as the unified LLM gateway in app/services/llm.py. Add: `uv add litellm`. LLMService class: generate(prompt: str, system: str = '', model: str | None = None, stream: bool = False) -> str | AsyncGenerator[str, None]. Model defaults to settings.LITELLM_DEFAULT_MODEL. When stream=False: call litellm.acompletion(), return full response string. When stream=True: call litellm.acompletion(stream=True), return async generator yielding token strings from choices[0].delta.content. Model routing: ollama/model routes to OLLAMA_URL, openai/model requires OPENAI_API_KEY, anthropic/model requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, gemini/model requires GOOGLE_API_KEY. stories.title: Layer linter, boundary validation, and CI Makefile
stories.description: Implement mechanical architecture enforcement. (1) backend/tools/layer_linter.py: use the ast module to parse all .py files under app/. For each file, determine its layer from its path (e.g. app/services/* = service layer, app/routers/* = router layer). Check each import statement: if a lower-layer module imports from a higher-layer module, emit an error with remediation text e.g. 'Layer violation in app/services/qa.py: service layer cannot import from app/routers/. (2) Boundary validation checker in tools/boundary_checker.py: parse all FastAPI route functions using ast, check that all parameters annotated as request bodies use Pydantic BaseModel subclasses, not dict or Any. (3) Add ruff rules to pyproject.toml [tool.ruff.lint]: add 'PL' (pylint) and custom ignore list. Add a ruff per-file-ignores rule. (4) Update Makefile at repo root with `make ci` target that runs in sequence: cd backend && uv sync, uv run ruff check ., uv run python tools/layer_linter.py, uv run python tools/boundary_checker.py, uv run pytest, cd frontend && npm run build, npx tsc --noEmit. (5) Create .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs `make ci` on push.

GET /study/history: query param days (default 90). Returns [{date: YYYY-MM-DD, cards_reviewed: int, study_time_minutes: int}] for each day in the range that has at least one session. Write tests/test_study_stats.py: create fixture data (sessions, reviewed cards), call stats endpoint, assert correct aggregation.

stories.title: RAGAS evaluation runner, eval_runs storage, and GET /monitoring/evals
stories.description: Create the evaluation pipeline. Add to evals requirements (create evals/pyproject.toml with `uv init`): `uv add ragas datasets`. Create evals/run_eval.py as a CLI script using argparse: --dataset (book|paper|conversation|notes|code), --model (default ollama/mistral), --backend-url (default http://localhost:8000). Script flow: load JSONL file from evals/golden/{dataset}.jsonl. For each row: POST to {backend-url}/qa with question, document_ids=None (all content). Collect {question, answer, contexts (top retrieved chunks), ground_truth}. After all rows: call ragas.evaluate(Dataset.from_list(samples), metrics=[faithfulness, answer_relevance, context_precision, context_recall]). Also compute hit_rate_5: for each question, check if ground_truth_answer substring appears in the text of any of the top-5 retrieved chunks. Compute mrr: rank of first chunk containing ground_truth. POST results to {backend-url}/monitoring/evals/store (add this endpoint). Print results table to stdout. Backend: POST /monitoring/evals/store accepts EvalRunCreate(dataset_name, model_used, hit_rate_5, mrr, faithfulness, answer_relevance, context_precision, context_recall). Inserts into eval_runs table. GET /monitoring/evals: returns last 10 eval_runs per dataset ordered by run_at desc. Frontend (Monitoring tab): add 'Eval Runs' section after the traces table. Fetch GET /monitoring/evals. Render a shadcn Table: Dataset, Run At, HR@5, MRR, Faithfulness. Row background: red if HR@5 < 0.5, green if > 0.7.

stories.title: Code repository ingestion with tree-sitter and call graph
stories.description: Extend ingestion to support code repositories. Add: `uv add tree-sitter tree-sitter-python tree-sitter-javascript tree-sitter-typescript tree-sitter-go tree-sitter-rust`. Create app/services/code_parser.py with CodeParser class: detect language from file extension (.py, .js, .ts, .go, .rs). Use tree-sitter Language and Parser to build the AST. Extract function and class definitions: for each function/class node extract name, docstring (if first child is a string literal), parameter names, start_line, end_line. Each function/class becomes its own chunk with metadata: file_path, language, function_name, class_name, start_line, end_line. Handle directory/zip upload: walk file tree, skip .git, node_modules, __pycache__, binary files (check by trying UTF-8 decode). Classify as content_type='code'. In classify_node (ingestion workflow): detect code format by checking chunk metadata for function_name field. In entity_extract_node: build call graph — scan each function body text for names that match other extracted function names, create CO_OCCURS edges in Kuzu labeled 'calls'. Add GET /graph/{document_id}?type=call_graph: filter Kuzu CO_OCCURS edges where relation_label='calls'. Frontend (Viz tab): add a 'View' toggle in the controls panel (S16b) — 'Knowledge Graph' vs 'Call Graph'. Call graph only visible for code documents. Fetches the ?type=call_graph endpoint.

stories.title: Colorized dev log script for backend and frontend
stories.description: Add a make logs target and a supporting shell script scripts/dev-logs.sh that runs both backend and frontend in a single terminal with color-coded, prefixed output for easy debugging. Requirements: (1) Backend lines prefixed with a cyan [BACKEND] label; frontend lines prefixed with a green [FRONTEND] label. (2) Backend log level forced to DEBUG via LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG env var so all ingestion pipeline stages are visible. (3) Use stdbuf -oL to disable output buffering so lines appear in real time. (4) The script must forward SIGINT (Ctrl-C) to both child processes and wait for clean shutdown. (5) Add make logs to the Makefile that calls bash scripts/dev-logs.sh. (6) Add a note in the Quickstart section of the README pointing to make logs as the recommended way to watch ingestion. Use ANSI escape codes directly — no external dependencies beyond bash and standard macOS/Linux tools. Test on macOS zsh.

stories.title: Fix LiteLLM/Ollama classify_node error crashing document ingestion
stories.description: Ingestion fails with an ASGI exception during classify_node when LiteLLM calls Ollama (mistral). Investigate the traceback: read app/workflows/ingestion.py classify_node, app/services/llm.py (or wherever the LiteLLM call is made), and app/config.py for OLLAMA_BASE_URL and MODEL settings. Common causes: (a) classify_node calling a sync LiteLLM function inside an async context without run_in_executor, (b) Ollama not running or wrong base URL, (c) model name mismatch (e.g. 'mistral' vs 'ollama/mistral'). Fix the root cause — do NOT just catch and swallow the exception. If the issue is sync-in-async, wrap with asyncio.get_event_loop().run_in_executor(None, ...) or switch to litellm.acompletion. Add a startup health-check log line that pings the configured Ollama URL and logs a clear WARNING if unreachable, so users know early. Add or update the existing test in tests/test_ingest.py to mock the LiteLLM call and assert classify_node returns a valid content_type without hitting the network. Additionally, fix a Kuzu file lock conflict that crashes the backend on startup when another process already holds the lock on the production graph.kuzu file. This happens when pytest runs while the dev backend is also running. Fix: in conftest.py (create if missing), add a session-scoped fixture that sets DATA_DIR to a pytest tmp_path so all tests use an isolated temp directory for SQLite, LanceDB, and Kuzu — never the production ~/.luminary path. Ensure the KuzuService, LanceDB, and SQLite init code all respect the DATA_DIR from settings so the fixture override is sufficient. Document in README: stop the dev backend before running pytest, or rely on the isolated fixture which makes it safe to run tests alongside a live backend.

stories.title: Delete document UI, cascade cleanup, and seed data removal script
stories.description: Two related problems: (1) No way to delete a single document from the UI — stuck or partial uploads have no escape hatch. (2) Old test seed data present in the database. Fix both. Backend: verify DELETE /documents/{id} exists and cascades — must delete rows from chunks, sections, LanceDB embeddings, FTS5 index, and Kuzu graph nodes. If missing or incomplete, implement fully. Add scripts/seed_cleanup.py that connects to the SQLite DB, lists all documents, and deletes any whose title contains 'test', 'sample', 'seed', 'fixture', or 'untitled' (case-insensitive) — prints each deletion with id and title, requires --confirm flag to actually delete (dry-run by default). Frontend (Learning tab, document card): add a trash icon button (shadcn Button variant=ghost, size=icon) in the top-right corner of each DocumentCard. On click, show a shadcn AlertDialog: 'Delete [title]? This cannot be undone.' Confirm calls DELETE /documents/{id}, invalidates the TanStack Query cache for the documents list, and shows a toast. Works for documents in any stage including error or parsing.
